mnemonics: POSITIVE PHRASES: Preprocess Optimally, Select Informative Vectors, Employ Transformers, Analyze Sentiment (A mnemonic for the key steps: Preprocessing, Vectorization, Model Selection, and Analysis)
keyPoints: Text preprocessing (cleaning, tokenization, lemmatization) is critical for removing noise that can confuse the model.
Choosing the right text representation (e.g., TF-IDF, Word2Vec, BERT embeddings) dramatically impacts model performance.
Fine-tuning a pre-trained transformer model (like BERT) on your specific dataset is often the most effective modern approach.
commonMistakes: Ignoring the context of words (e.g., 'not good' is negative, but a bag-of-words model might miss this).
Failing to address class imbalance in the training data, which can lead to a model that always predicts the majority sentiment.
